title: Update API Objects in Place Using kubectl patch — Alternate forms of the kubectl patch command
text:
The
kubectl patch
command takes YAML or JSON. It can take the patch as a file or
directly on the command line.
Create a file named
patch-file.json
that has this content:
{
"spec"
:
{
"template"
:
{
"spec"
:
{
"containers"
:
[
{
"name"
:
"patch-demo-ctr-2"
,
"image"
:
"redis"
}
]
}
}
}
}
The following commands are equivalent:
kubectl patch deployment patch-demo --patch-file patch-file.yaml
kubectl patch deployment patch-demo --patch
'spec:\n template:\n  spec:\n   containers:\n   - name: patch-demo-ctr-2\n     image: redis'
kubectl patch deployment patch-demo --patch-file patch-file.json
kubectl patch deployment patch-demo --patch
'{"spec": {"template": {"spec": {"containers": [{"name": "patch-demo-ctr-2","image": "redis"}]}}}}'
